name: Vetala
description: A Vetala is a revenant or demonic spirit in Hindu mythology. Vetalas are often described as mischievous and malevolent spirits that inhabit corpses. They are capable of possessing and reanimating the dead, using them as puppets to terrorize the living. Vetalas are known for their cunning and their ability to answer riddles, although they often test the wit of those who seek their knowledge. They are associated with cremation grounds, cemeteries, and other liminal spaces between life and death. Vetalas are not inherently evil, but their nature is often amoral and unpredictable. They can be compelled to serve powerful sorcerers or ascetics, but they are also known to trick and betray those who try to control them. Their power lies in their knowledge of the past, present, and future, and their ability to manipulate the physical world through the reanimated corpses they inhabit. Stories of Vetalas often involve complex moral dilemmas and tests of character for the protagonists who encounter them. They represent the unsettling aspects of death and the potential for the dead to influence the world of the living. They are often depicted as having a grotesque appearance, reflecting their association with decay and the macabre.
subtitle: Corpse-dwelling spirit of the night.
appearance: Vetalas are generally described as having a grotesque and decaying appearance, reflecting their association with corpses. They may have bloated bodies, sunken eyes, and decaying flesh. Their skin is often described as being pale or greenish. They can also shapeshift, taking on the form of animals or other humans. Sometimes described as having red eyes and long, matted hair.
habitat: Vetalas are primarily found in cremation grounds, cemeteries, abandoned buildings, and other places associated with death and decay. They prefer dark and isolated locations, where they can remain undisturbed. They are often associated with banyan trees and other locations considered spiritually significant.
behavior: Vetalas are typically mischievous and malevolent spirits. They enjoy tormenting the living and testing their wit. They are known for their cunning and their ability to answer riddles, but they often use their knowledge to trick and manipulate others. They are fiercely independent and resistant to being controlled, but they can be compelled to serve powerful sorcerers or ascetics.

keyMyths.title: The Vetala Panchavimshati
keyMyths.description: The Vetala Panchavimshati (Twenty-Five Tales of the Vetala) is a collection of stories in which King Vikramaditya attempts to capture a Vetala. Each time, the Vetala tells a story with a riddle, and if Vikramaditya answers correctly, the Vetala escapes back to its tree.

sources.author: Somadeva
sources.description: A collection of Indian folk tales, including stories featuring Vetalas.
sources.text: A collection of Indian folk tales, including stories featuring Vetalas.

sources.author: Unknown
